title: POST /api/auth/register — create new user account
description: Implement the user registration endpoint that accepts name, email, and password, creates a new user in the database, and returns a JWT token so the user is immediately logged in.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/register with valid data returns 201 with user object and access_token
Duplicate email returns 400 with message 'Email already registered'
Missing required fields return 422 validation error
Password is stored as bcrypt hash, never plaintext
Newly created user can immediately log in with their credentials

steps.details: Create src/api/schemas/auth.py. Define RegisterRequest(BaseModel) with fields: name (str, min_length=1), email (EmailStr), password (str, min_length=8). Define AuthResponse(BaseModel) with fields: access_token (str), token_type (str, default='bearer'), user (UserResponse). Define UserResponse(BaseModel) with fields: id (str), email (str), name (str), weight_unit (str). Use model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True) on UserResponse.

steps.description: Create the auth router and register endpoint
steps.details: Create src/api/routers/auth.py. Create APIRouter with prefix='/api/auth', tags=['auth']. Implement POST /register endpoint: accept RegisterRequest body, check if email already exists (query db for user with matching email, return 400 if found), hash password with hash_password(), create User model instance, add to db and commit, create JWT token with create_access_token({'sub': user.id}), return AuthResponse with token and UserResponse.

steps.description: Register the auth router in main.py
steps.details: In src/api/main.py, import the auth router and call app.include_router(auth_router). Ensure it's included after the database initialization.

Use curl or the FastAPI /docs page: POST to http://localhost:8000/api/auth/register with body {"name": "Test User", "email": "test@example.com", "password": "password123"}. Verify: status 201, response contains access_token and user object, user.id is a UUID string, no hashed_password in response. Then POST again with same email and verify 400 error.

technicalNotes: Use EmailStr from pydantic for email validation — requires pydantic[email] or email-validator package
Return 201 status code for successful creation using response_model and status_code=201 in the decorator
